pi_items.source: Consistent across task.json, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh scripts; used as working directory configuration
Consistent across task.json, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh scripts; task description specifies Desktop location
Matches task description exactly; verified in export_result.sh directory structure checks
Web search confirmed osha.gov is the official Occupational Safety and Health Administration website (https://www.osha.gov/) with publicly available PDF documents for workplace safety

Web search confirmed fema.gov is the official Federal Emergency Management Agency website (https://www.fema.gov/). Task mentions both fema.gov and usfa.fema.gov (U.S. Fire Administration under FEMA) as valid sources for fire safety PDFs
Task description explicitly states 'download at least 3 distinct PDF reference documents'; metadata value matches exactly

privileged_info_summary: The task requires users to configure Microsoft Edge settings and download real PDF documents from verified government websites: osha.gov (Occupational Safety and Health Administration) for workplace safety documents and fema.gov/usfa.fema.gov (Federal Emergency Management Agency and U.S. Fire Administration) for fire safety documents. A minimum of 3 distinct PDF documents must be downloaded and organized into three subdirectories (OSHA, FEMA, and General) within /home/ga/Documents/InspectionDocs. All metadata claims regarding required domains, directory structure, and minimum PDF count have been verified against authoritative sources and match the task description.
